gates.title: Provider runtime maps Anthropic to Claude Code and OpenAI to Codex without BYOK/API conflation
evidence_required: Provider runtime exposes Anthropic/OpenAI as runtime-account choices
OpenAI maps to Codex and Anthropic maps to Claude Code

gates.title: Desktop-managed installs use only managed Claude/Codex runtimes
evidence_required: Desktop-managed Codex detection requires the managed binary plus native vendor
Desktop-managed Codex never falls back to a host global npm install
Desktop-managed Codex sync ignores global PATH Codex and only runs a managed binary with native vendor
Headless Codex runner requires the managed wrapper plus native vendor before marking the backend available
Desktop-managed Claude detection ignores stale global and persisted host paths

gates.title: Sessions, automation runs, and crons persist provider/client metadata
evidence_required: cron wrapper records the selected automation provider/backend/runtime_snapshot

gates.title: Brain repo and Desktop bundle ship Codex vendor tarballs while npm stays publishable
evidence_required: npm pack intentionally includes only codex/openai-codex-0.133.0.tgz so the public npm publish stays below registry limits
repo release artifacts retain darwin, linux, and win32 native Codex tarballs for Desktop bundling
Desktop release artifact verification confirms the native Codex vendor tarball for each platform
release readiness blocks incomplete public surfaces before tag publish

gates.title: Public release surfaces and integration artifacts are aligned for v7.26.0
evidence_required: README, llms, homepage, blog index, blog post, changelog page, and sitemap reference v7.26.0
Claude plugin, ClawHub skill, and OpenClaw package metadata reference 7.26.0
Release smoke and readiness checks validate the v7.26.0 contract before tag publish
